source: Basic Conditions of Employment Act 75 of 1997 — Section 6(3) — annual determination by Minister of Employment and Labour
data_status: PROVISIONAL — figure estimated at approximately +5.4% above the 2025 threshold of R254,371.67/year. MUST be replaced with confirmed figure from Government Gazette published by the Department of Employment and Labour (typically February/March 2026). Update this file and re-run StatutoryRuleSetLoader.LoadAllAsync() to overwrite the Firestore document.

history.note: 2024→2025 increase was +5.5%. 2026 provisional assumes similar CPI uplift.

bcea_protections_below_threshold.description: Employees earning BELOW the threshold are entitled to all BCEA protections. Employees earning AT OR ABOVE the threshold may contract out of certain protections with their employer.
protections_applicable: Section 9: Maximum ordinary hours of work (45 hours/week)
Section 10: Overtime — written agreement required, 1.5× rate for OT, 2× for Sundays/public holidays
Section 11: Compressed working week agreement limits
Section 12: Averaging of hours agreement limits
Section 14: Meal interval (30-60 minutes after 5 continuous hours)
Section 15: Daily and weekly rest periods
Section 16: Pay for work on Sundays
Section 18(3): Public holiday work — must pay agreed wage plus ordinary day's pay
protections_that_can_be_varied_above_thr: Ordinary hours of work can exceed 45 hours/week by written agreement
Overtime limits can be varied by written agreement
Meal interval can be reduced below 30 minutes by written agreement

compliance_rules: Always use the MONTHLY threshold for monthly payroll employees
For weekly payroll: use weekly_threshold per pay period
Compare employee's TOTAL monthly remuneration (including allowances, overtime, etc.) against monthly_threshold
If employee earns at or above threshold, note that BCEA protections may be varied — but must still be by written agreement
Even above-threshold employees retain all LRA rights (unfair dismissal, unfair labour practice)
NMW always applies regardless of threshold — no employee may earn below NMW
